title: polystichum subquinquefidum
intro: Polystichum subquinquefidum, also known as the five-lobed spleenwort, is a species of fern that belongs to the family Dryopteridaceae. Traditionally, it has been used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The plant is believed to have health benefits such as reducing fever, soothing digestive issues, and alleviating respiratory problems. The fern is typically prepared as a tea, decoction, or tincture for herbal remedies, although more research is needed to confirm its effectiveness.
